integration.title: Solar Cover - Global Settings
integration.description: These settings apply to all cover zones. All fields are optional.

data.inactive_position: Rest position when sun is not active

data_description.weather_entity: Used to detect rain and wind. Leave empty to skip weather checks.
data_description.wind_threshold: Covers will retract automatically when wind speed exceeds this value (m/s). Leave empty to ignore wind.
data_description.min_temp: Covers stay open below this temperature so the room can warm up (°C).
data_description.inactive_position: How far open or closed the covers sit when the sun is not hitting the window (0 = fully open, 100 = fully closed).
data_description.override_duration: After you move a cover manually, automation stays paused for this many minutes.
data_description.cloud_entity: A sensor reporting cloud coverage as a percentage (0-100). Leave empty to skip. Example: sensor.openweathermap_cloud_coverage.
data_description.cloud_threshold: Covers stay retracted when cloud coverage is above this percentage. Useful when no radiation sensor is available.
data_description.radiation_entity: A sensor reporting solar irradiance in W/m². Leave empty to skip. Takes precedence over cloud coverage when both are configured. Example: sensor.zamg_global_radiation.
data_description.radiation_threshold: Covers stay retracted when solar radiation is below this value (W/m²). A clear summer midday is typically 800-1000 W/m². Start with 120.
data_description.stability_delay_minutes: Wait this many minutes before reacting to a weather change, so covers don't open and close repeatedly on a partly-cloudy or gusty day. 0 disables the delay.
data_description.stability_delay_on_worsening: Wait before retracting when conditions worsen (wind picks up, clouds roll in). Turn off to react immediately.
data_description.stability_delay_on_recovery: Wait before re-deploying when conditions improve (sun returns). Turn off to react immediately.

zone.title: Add a Cover Zone
zone.description: Give this zone a name, assign the cover devices it controls, and select the cover type. The next screen will show only the settings relevant to your selection.

data_description.name: A short label for this zone, for example: Living room south.
data_description.cover_entities: The cover devices that move together in this zone. Leave empty to observe without controlling anything.
data_description.cover_type: The physical type of cover. This determines which geometry is used to calculate the position.

zone_configure.title: Zone Settings
zone_configure.description: Configure the geometry and position limits for this zone. Only the fields relevant to your cover type are shown.

data.fov_left: How far left the sun shines in
data.fov_right: How far right the sun shines in
data.glare_depth: How far into the room to shade
data.attach_height: Height of awning rail above floor

data_description.azimuth: The compass direction your window faces (0 = North, 90 = East, 180 = South, 270 = West).
data_description.fov_left: How many degrees to the left of the window center the sun can still shine in. Start with 90 and reduce if a wall blocks part of the view.
data_description.fov_right: How many degrees to the right of the window center the sun can still shine in. Start with 90 and reduce if a wall blocks part of the view.
data_description.elevation_threshold: Covers only deploy when the sun is at least this many degrees above the horizon. Pre-filled from your location.
data_description.window_height: The height of the glass area from floor to top, in metres.
data_description.glare_depth: For blinds: how far into the room to keep free of direct sunlight, in metres. For awnings: how far out onto the terrace the shadow should reach, in metres.
data_description.min_position: The cover will never go below this position while shading. Set to 30% to always keep some daylight in.
data_description.max_position: The cover will never go above this position while shading. Set to 80% to always keep some view.
data_description.attach_height: How high above the floor the awning rail is mounted, in metres.
data_description.awn_length: How far out the awning extends when fully open, measured horizontally in metres.
data_description.awn_angle: The downward tilt angle of the awning from horizontal, in degrees. Typical: 10-20°.
data_description.slat_width: The width of each individual slat, in millimetres.
data_description.slat_spacing: The distance from the center of one slat to the next, in millimetres. Must not exceed slat width.
data_description.tilt_range: Single: slats tilt from fully open to fully closed (0-90°). Bidirectional: slats can also tilt the other direction.

error.spacing_exceeds_width: Slat spacing must not exceed the slat width.

init.title: Edit Settings
init.description: Update the settings below and press Submit to apply changes.

data.inactive_position: Rest position when sun is not active
data.hysteresis: Minimum position change to move covers
data.fov_left: How far left the sun shines in
data.fov_right: How far right the sun shines in
data.glare_depth: How far into the room to shade

data_description.weather_entity: Used to detect rain and wind. Leave empty to skip weather checks.
data_description.wind_threshold: Covers retract automatically when wind exceeds this speed (m/s).
data_description.min_temp: Covers stay retracted below this temperature to let in warmth (°C).
data_description.inactive_position: Position (0-100%) when the sun is not hitting the window.
data_description.override_duration: After you move a cover manually, automation stays paused for this many minutes.
data_description.hysteresis: Only move covers when the new position differs by at least this percentage. Prevents motor chatter. Default: 3%.
data_description.cloud_entity: A sensor reporting cloud coverage as a percentage (0-100). Leave empty to skip.
data_description.cloud_threshold: Covers stay retracted when cloud coverage is above this percentage.
data_description.radiation_entity: A sensor reporting solar irradiance in W/m². Leave empty to skip. Takes precedence over cloud coverage when both are configured.
data_description.radiation_threshold: Covers stay retracted when solar radiation is below this value (W/m²). Start with 120.
data_description.stability_delay_minutes: Wait this many minutes before reacting to a weather change, so covers don't open and close repeatedly on a partly-cloudy or gusty day. 0 disables the delay.
data_description.stability_delay_on_worsening: Wait before retracting when conditions worsen (wind picks up, clouds roll in). Turn off to react immediately.
data_description.stability_delay_on_recovery: Wait before re-deploying when conditions improve (sun returns). Turn off to react immediately.
data_description.cover_entities: The cover devices that move together in this zone.
data_description.cover_type: The physical type of cover determines which geometry is used.
data_description.fov_left: Degrees left of center where the sun first hits this window. Start with 90.
data_description.fov_right: Degrees right of center where the sun last hits this window. Start with 90.
data_description.elevation_threshold: Covers only deploy when the sun is above this angle (°).
data_description.window_height: Floor-to-ceiling height of the glazed area in metres.
data_description.glare_depth: How far into the room you want to keep shaded (metres).
data_description.min_position: The cover will never go below this position while shading. Set to 30% to always keep some daylight in.
data_description.max_position: The cover will never go above this position while shading. Set to 80% if you always want some view through the window.

geometry_vertical.title: Blind Dimensions
geometry_vertical.description: Enter the window size and how far into the room you want to keep free of direct sunlight.

data.glare_depth: How far into the room to shade

data_description.window_height: The height of the glass area from floor to top, in metres.
data_description.glare_depth: How far from the window you want to keep free of direct sunlight, in metres. A desk 1.5 m from the window would need at least 1.5 m here.

geometry_horizontal.title: Awning Settings
geometry_horizontal.description: Enter the physical dimensions of your awning and how far out onto the terrace you want to keep in shade.

data.glare_depth: How far out onto the terrace to shade
data.attach_height: Height of awning rail above floor

data_description.glare_depth: The awning extends until its shadow reaches this far from the wall, in metres. If your outdoor table sits 3 m from the wall, set this to 3 m.
data_description.attach_height: Height of the awning rail above the floor (metres).
data_description.awn_length: Maximum horizontal projection of the awning when fully extended (metres).
data_description.awn_angle: Downward angle of the awning from horizontal (degrees). Typical: 10-20°.

geometry_tilt.title: Venetian Blind Settings
geometry_tilt.description: Enter the slat dimensions for the tilt calculation.

data_description.slat_width: Width of each slat in millimetres.
data_description.slat_spacing: The distance from the center of one slat to the center of the next, in millimetres. Must be less than or equal to slat width.
data_description.tilt_range: Single: slats tilt from fully open to fully closed (0-90°). Bidirectional: slats can also tilt past horizontal in both directions.

error.spacing_exceeds_width: Slat spacing must not exceed the slat width.
